data.text: A FELISIAN WORLD- HAS A GREEN CAST WITH MUCH WATER. AT 149.60 IT
WAS MOSTLY GRASSY AND HILLY WITH TREES AND BUSHES WITH YELLOW
FOILAGE. AT 252.22 IT WAS ROLLING TERRITORY WITH A RED AND GREEN
TINT, A COUPLE OF HILLS WERE IN THE DISTANCE, BIRDS WERE SIGHTED.
AT 25.92 IT WAS HILLY TERRITORY FORESTED WITH STRANGLY SHAPED
TREES WITH RED AND GREEN FOILAGE. SKINNYMON EPOC 6011 602.690
UNLIKE EMPTOR, THIS FELISIAN WORLD IS MOSTLY WATER. AT 150,61
THERE IS WHAT APPEARS TO BE A HUGE CRATER COVERED IN YELLOW
VEGETATION. 89,44-TWO ISLANDS SEPARATED BY A STRAIT. THE MOST
INTERESTING FEATURE SO FAR, A MONUMENT-VALLEY-ESQUE FEATURE, CAN
BE FOUND AT 142,46. IT SEEMS TO BE VOLCANIC, MAYBE SOME ELEVATED
CALDERA THINGY...I'M NOT A GEOLOGIST, BY PARSIS!
147,18 IS ALSO WORTH A LOOK FOR THE VAST AMOUNTS OF FROZEN ISLANDS
...A GREAT PLACE TO TROT AROUND. 88,1: PROBABLY AN EXCELLENT
BIRDCATCHING SPOT. WHEN I LANDED THAT WERE AT LEAST 4 BIRDS ON
THE GROUND IN FRONT OF THE CAPSULE! TALK ABOUT AN EASY SNACK!
MEANWHILE, IF YOU LIKE ICY VALLEYS, 87,5 IS A DEFINITE MUST-LAND
YOU'LL FIND YOURSELF ON THE LIP OF A SPECTACULAR CANYON. WORTH
SOME EXPLORATION. RYAN GJK E6011.789.151
THOUGH THE ANGLE SHOULD BE RIGHT, I WASN'T ABLE TO SEE 'THE GRID' FROM
THE SURFACE. IT MUST JUST BE A SYSTEM GLITCH. 316.60 HAS SOME
IMPRESSIVE ROCKS STICKING OUT OF THE WATER (TOUGH SWIM TO GET THERE) -
MORE VOLCANIC REMNANTS? VISITING ANY DAYTIME AREA IS RECOMMENDED; THE
SKY REMINDS ME OF SURICRASIA, AND THE WATER IS CLEAR LIKE NOTHING I HAVE
EVER SEEN. LOTS OF BIRDS, TOO (TWOTWOS, 6012:247.72)
